name: Johnny Ripe
signature: The Peninsula's hinterland pie stop — proper beef pies, a sweets cabinet worth detouring for, and a garden café out the back
editorNote:
Johnny Ripe is the pie stop the Peninsula has needed for years, and the one visitors end up returning to on every subsequent trip. The savoury case is serious — slow-cooked beef pie, a lamb-and-rosemary number, a sausage roll that respects the form — and the sweets cabinet is the other reason to come: lemon tart, meringues, a rotating set of seasonal fruit tarts that routinely sell out before closing.

The garden café behind the shop is the right place to eat the pie you just bought. Grab a hot pie at the counter, order a coffee, take it outside, let the kids run between the trees. This is the kind of Peninsula stop that turns a winery morning into a properly rounded afternoon.

Buy more sweets than you planned. Leave one in the car for the drive home.
